title: Predicting emergence of crystals from amorphous precursors with deep learning potentials

data_scope.subcells_per_system: 10^3 to 10^7 depending on n_grid and stoichiometry filters
data_scope.amorphous_validation_error: 38 meV per atom vs DFT across thousands of amorphous binary structures

core_conclusion: A computational framework (a2c) using universal deep learning interatomic potentials predicts, with high accuracy, which crystal polymorphs nucleate from amorphous precursors across 12 diverse inorganic material systems. By sampling local structural motifs in amorphous configurations and relaxing periodic subcells under a GNN force field, a2c achieves up to ~6-fold improvement in polymorph selectivity over random structure search, and resolves previously unsolved crystal structures.
why_it_matters: Breaks a long-standing barrier in materials synthesis prediction by combining nucleation theory with universal GNN potentials; demonstrates universality across oxides, nitrides, carbides, fluorides, chalcogenides and metallic glasses; resolves outstanding crystal structure problems (BiBO3 polymorph II); runs on a single GPU making it practical for high-throughput materials discovery.
why_nature: Introduces a new computational paradigm (amorphous-basin-guided local motif search) with validated universality across 12 chemical systems; advances the universal machine learning interatomic potential frontier; fully open data and code release; broad implications for materials science, geology, astrophysics and astrobiology.
added_value_to_my_research: Uncertainty-driven active learning loop for interatomic potentials -> analogous active geographic data collection for underrepresented regions in spatiotemporal models
Local motif seeds global crystal order -> local neighborhood features predict emergent urban structure without city-wide optimization
Multi-potential ensemble consensus for uncertain predictions -> cross-model disagreement as spatially resolved uncertainty map for spatial ML
Two-stage generate-then-score pipeline (a2c + stability scoring) -> constrained generate-then-score for EV/facility placement optimization outperforming global random search
Heterogeneous graph with type-conditioned message passing for bulk/surface environments -> architectural template for GeoAI GNNs with semantically heterogeneous urban node types

figures.caption: Computational pipeline: energy landscape with amorphous basin, MQMD workflow, subcell extraction and periodic relaxation, and predicted crystal structures for 12 inorganic systems matching experimental polymorphs
Benchmarking a2c vs. random structure search (RSS) across 7 systems showing the acceleration factor (1.2x to 6x) in identifying the correct crystallization product
